title: Never reuse a nonce under one key
description: Guarantee each (key, nonce) pair is used once, because nonce reuse with AEAD/stream ciphers is catastrophic for confidentiality and integrity.
body: Use whenever you call an AEAD or stream cipher. Do not derive nonces from low-entropy or repeating sources, and avoid a fixed/zero nonce or a counter that resets when the process restarts. Actions: use a strictly increasing counter persisted per key, or random nonces only when the nonce width is large enough to make collision negligible; rotate the key well before the nonce space is exhausted; consider a misuse-resistant mode (AES-GCM-SIV) when uniqueness is hard to guarantee. Validation signals include a test asserting no nonce repeats across restarts and a key-rotation trigger before exhaustion. Risk boundary: a single nonce reuse can leak plaintext XOR and forge tags; treat any path that can repeat a nonce as a release blocker.
